title: Bin deodoriser discs
subtitle: Compressed bicarbonate of soda discs with essential oils and citric acid for bin odour control
excerpt: These dry discs sit in the bottom of a bin liner and neutralise odours. Bicarbonate of soda absorbs acidic smell compounds; a small amount of citric acid reacts gently when damp to release the essential oil blend. Add water carefully when mixing: too much starts a vigorous bicarbonate-and-acid reaction before the discs are moulded.
sourceNotes: Bin deodoriser discs: bicarbonate of soda (sodium bicarbonate) as primary odour absorber; citric acid at lower ratio than bath bombs to produce slow rather than rapid reaction. Cornstarch as binder. Essential oils: lavender, tea tree, peppermint. Pressed into silicone ice cube or bath-bomb moulds. Dry 24 hours before use.

recipe.batchNotes: Double or triple the batch and fill more moulds. The mixture must be pressed into moulds quickly once water is added.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 24 hours to dry and harden before removing from moulds.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in a sealed container away from moisture. If the discs are stored in a damp environment they will react and lose their deodorising power. Keep in a sealed tin or jar.

glossaryTerms.definition: Sodium bicarbonate; a mild alkali that neutralises acidic odour compounds such as those produced by rotting food. Different from washing soda (sodium carbonate), which is much more alkaline.

A mild organic acid that reacts with bicarbonate of soda when damp to produce carbon dioxide. In these discs the reaction is gentle and slow; it releases the essential oil fragrance when the disc gets damp in the bin.

content.text: neutralises acidic smell compounds. The small amount of

in these discs does not create a rapid bath-bomb reaction; at this ratio it reacts slowly when damp, releasing the essential oils into the air around the bin. The key to making the discs without mishap is adding the water very slowly, drop by drop, and working quickly once the mixture starts to bind.

items.prepNote: Lower ratio than bath bombs; add dry with the bicarb
Binder; keeps the discs firm once dried
Add to dry mix and stir before adding water
Add very slowly, drop by drop, to activate binding without triggering full reaction

content.text: Combine bicarbonate of soda, citric acid, and cornstarch in the mixing bowl. Stir well. Add all essential oils and stir again until evenly distributed.

Spray or drizzle the water into the dry mixture very slowly, a few drops at a time, stirring after each addition. The mixture will fizz slightly; stop adding water the moment it holds together when squeezed. It should feel like damp sand. Press firmly into silicone moulds. Tap the mould on the work surface to compact. Leave to dry uncovered for 24 hours.

After 24 hours the discs should be firm. Flex the silicone mould gently to release. If the discs crumble, they needed more water; if they expanded and cracked, they had too much. Store in a sealed tin.
Drop 1 to 2 discs into the bottom of the bin under the liner, or place directly in a recycling bin. Replace when the scent has faded, usually every 2 to 3 weeks.
6 months in a sealed container away from moisture. Keep in a tin with a tight-fitting lid.
